content.problemStatement:
Given an array check if it represents a min-heap or not.

Print "Yes" if it represents a min-heap. Otherwise, print "No".
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains an integer n (the number of elements in the array). The second line contains n space‑separated integers representing the array in level‑order (0‑based index).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "Yes" if the given array satisfies the min‑heap property (for every index i, arr[i] ≤ arr[2*i+1] and arr[i] ≤ arr[2*i+2] when those children exist), otherwise output "No".

sampleTestCases.explanation: First array: every parent is ≤ its children, so it satisfies the min‑heap property → Yes. Second array: parent at index 0 is 15 while its left child at index 1 is 10 (<15) → violation → No.
Array is strictly increasing, therefore each parent ≤ its children, satisfying the min‑heap property.
Root 25 > left child 20, so the heap property is broken.

solution.approach: The array is given in level‑order, which is exactly the representation used by binary heaps. For each index i we only need to check its left child (2*i+1) and right child (2*i+2) if they exist. If any child is smaller than its parent the min‑heap property is violated. Scanning the array once is sufficient, giving O(n) time per test case and O(n) auxiliary space for the input array.
solution.timeComplexity: O(n) per test case, O(total N) for the whole input.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(n) per test case for storing the array.

code.Java: import java.io.*;import java.util.*;public class Main{public static void main(String[] args)throws Exception{String data=new String(System.in.readAllBytes());data=data.replace("\\n"," ").replace("\\r"," ");StringTokenizer st=new StringTokenizer(data);int T=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());StringBuilder sb=new StringBuilder();while(T-- >0){int n=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());int[] a=new int[n];for(int i=0;i<n;i++)a[i]=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());boolean ok=true;for(int i=0;i<n && ok;i++){int l=2*i+1;int r=2*i+2;if(l<n && a[l]<a[i])ok=false;if(r<n && a[r]<a[i])ok=false;}sb.append(ok?"Yes":"No");if(T>0)sb.append('\n');}System.out.print(sb.toString());}}
